trimmedChitChat.startTrimmed: 00:00:00-00:11:42 — Tech check, intro greetings, kudos to Lorraine, banter about new phones.
trimmedChitChat.endTrimmed: 01:13:17-01:15:21 — Final goodbyes, follow-up logistics, sign-off chatter.

lessons.title: Why the AIP Diet Isn't Enough: Restrictive Diets and Hidden Inflammation
lessons.topicSummary: Rita's 'I am safe' nervous-system framing connects immune regulation to discussion. Naomi shares her physician perspective on minimal nutrition training. Rita introduces herself (Creighton FCP, NTP, RWS-trained), explains her shift to functional medicine. Critiques AIP as severely restrictive symptom management that fails to address root causes — poor digestion, vitamin/mineral deficiencies, co-infections, dysbiotic bacteria, parasites, yeast, blood-sugar dysregulation.

lessons.title: Case Study 1: PCOS and Endometriosis
lessons.topicSummary: 30-year-old client with PCOS and endometriosis. GI map revealed poor fat breakdown (high steatocrit), gluten reactivity, and dysbiotic bacteria correlated with low estrogen and progesterone. 12-week supplement protocol focused on fat digestion (beets, Beta Plus, Beta TCP) and microbial eradication. Result: first 30-day cycle without progesterone, 10 lbs lost, hair regrowth.

lessons.title: Case Study 2: Severe Endometriosis with Parasites and Lyme
lessons.topicSummary: 18-year-old post-excision with PMDD, chronic pain, hot flashes, Lyme history. GI map showed Morganella, Pseudomonas, Citrobacter, Blastocystis hominis, depleted Secretory IgA. Protocol: stomach-acid support, probiotic, eradication agents, parasite cleanse (passed several large tapeworms). Includes Naomi's clinical aside on Lyme/endometriosis correlation and Herxheimer reactions post-surgery.

lessons.title: Functional Testing, Protocol Timing, and Avoiding Long-Term Supplement Dependence
lessons.topicSummary: Q&A on Rita's training (Restorative Wellness Solutions), supplement selection, when she recommends GI map (~90% of clients). Optimal protocol timing — ideally 3 months pre-surgery. Four-month engagement model. Critical principle: neither restrictive diets nor supplement-heavy regimens should be long-term solutions; restore the body's own digestive function.

lessons.title: Restoring Fat Absorption and Whole-Fat Dairy
lessons.topicSummary: Naomi connects fat absorption to calming inflammation, parallels IV intralipids for recurrent pregnancy loss. Rita explains bile-salt protocol post-cholecystectomy and long-term sequelae of gallbladder removal. Whole-fat dairy and the 'war on fat'. Reframing healing as nourishment vs restriction.

lessons.title: Q&A: Managing Elevated Prolactin
lessons.topicSummary: Victoria's Q&A on elevated prolactin: confirming a true high (fasting, no nipple stimulation/intercourse), Vitex as natural option, cabergoline twice weekly, bromocriptine alternative, brain MRI thresholds, and Naomi's deprescribing principle for both supplements and extreme diets.

notes: APPROVED 2026-05-01. 6 lessons. Case studies split. Prolactin Q&A separated into its own lesson. All lessons contiguous: 702->1688->2230->3037->3744->4190->4397.
